observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to thoroughly review
all PPE items with individual acquisition costs below ₱50,000.00 that were
acquired and issued prior to CY 2024, and effect the required retrospective
reclassification and adjustments. This includes transferring the carrying
amounts to Prior Period Adjustment and restating the affected comparative
figures in the financial statements, in compliance with Sections 4.2 and 4.6 of
COA Circular No. 2022-004, as supplemented by COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to implement
the prescribed accounting treatments and documentation protocols moving
forward, including the proper use of Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS) for
applicable semi-expendable items, and ensure timely and accurate recording
based on the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2024-006 and its
annexes.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to coordinate with the
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to determine whether
the Accounts Payable totaling ₱428,893.60, aged from over two to 10 years, are
still valid claims and to ensure the accuracy of the liability account presented in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
accounting entries to correct the recording of the PPE items totaling
₱408,532.00, and adjust the corresponding depreciation expense accounts.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, refer to
the description of accounts as a guide for recording expenses in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, to avoid making erroneous
accounting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly adhere to the
accounting and reporting guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2024-
006. Specifically, tangible items costing below ₱50,000.00 and meeting the
definition of semi-expendable property should be:

Recorded under the appropriate semi-expendable inventory accounts
upon acquisition, and
Recognized as expense upon issuance to end-users, with proper
accounting entries reflected in the books of accounts.

We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant,
and all accountable officers agreed to ensure the preparation, maintenance, and
use of all required forms, registries, and reports for the proper tracking,
issuance, transfer, return, and disposal of semi-expendable properties, as
enumerated in Section 4.7 of COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly adhere to the
illustrative accounting entries prescribed under COA Circular Nos. 2012-002
and 2015-009 in recording the utilization of the unspent LDRRMF to ensure
proper accounting and accurate financial reporting of trust fund transactions.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant take into account
the budget revisions made during the year to ensure that the current year
appropriations for the LDRRMF are accurately disclosed in the Notes to FS as
of December 31.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to strengthen efforts to
collect delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaigns in
each barangay and sending Notices of Delinquency to all delinquent taxpayers
regardless of the amount due, to ensure fair enforcement and maximize revenue
collection.

We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to ensure full
compliance with the posting and publication requirements prescribed under
Section 254 of R.A. No. 7160.

Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to consider applying the
remedies for the collection of real property taxes, in accordance with the
pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

8.13.1 Cease the placement of funds in time deposits unless properly supported
by a detailed computation showing excess funds beyond the
Municipality’s normal operating and financial requirements, in
accordance with Sections 21 and 22 of COA Circular No. 92-382; and
8.13.2 Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to retrieve and submit complete
supporting documents for the existing time deposit account to validate
the legality and appropriateness of such investments.

We recommended that Management ensure strict adherence to the procurement
procedures prescribed under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, especially for
Negotiated Procurement under the "Two Failed Biddings" modality. This
includes requiring and retaining all mandated supporting documents, such as:

Updated Certificate of PhilGEPS Registration,
BAC recommendation to the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE),
HOPE’s approval of the award, and
Proof of posting of the Notice of Award, Contract/PO, and Notice to
Proceed.

We further recommended that Management ensure that all disbursements for
the project titled Civil Works for the Installation of Multi-Hazard Monitoring
Device (Closed-Circuit Television & Command Center), costing ₱3,378,422.82, are
supported by the required documents.

Lastly, we recommended that Management investigate the apparent irregularity
of supplier quotations predating the procurement notices to determine whether there
was prior disclosure or manipulation of procurement timelines. Appropriate
accountability measures should be implemented if impropriety is confirmed.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer agreed to
inform the contractor of the deficiencies noted and require them to either
perform the needed remedy works or refund the amount corresponding to the
deficiency noted, totaling ₱84,575.18.

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that future contractors for specialized projects such as rescue
boat fabrication hold a PCAB license and business registration
specifically covering marine or mechanical works.
Require the BAC to thoroughly assess the technical and legal eligibility of
bidders before awarding contracts, including verification of appropriate
license classifications.
Obtain technical certification or clearance from relevant regulatory
agencies such as MARINA or the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) to
validate project specifications and contractor capability.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer agreed to
inform the contractor of the deficiencies noted and require them to either
perform the needed remedy works or refund the amount corresponding to the
deficiency noted, totaling ₱146,864.77.

We recommended and the LDRRMC and LDRRMO agreed to review and
update the LDRRMFIP annually to include projects and activities chargeable to
prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-
002 dated September 12, 2012, to ensure the availability of funds for the timely
implementation of LDRRM programs, projects, and activities.

1. We recommended that Management strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system and earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.
2. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.
observations.managementAction: Management had yet to fully comply with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
3. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to promptly update, balance, and certify the collections, deposits, and disbursements on the cashbooks, ensure the correctness and accuracy of entries recorded therein, and henceforth adhere to the existing regulations on the handling, custody, and disposition of the cashbooks.
4. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors to ensure the fairness of the presentation of the cash and cash equivalent account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Updating of books was ongoing. Reconciliation was still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
8. We recommended that the Municipal personnel who are still employed in the Municipality be required to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances, otherwise, the sanction of withholding of honoraria provided under existing COA rules and regulations will have to be imposed.
9. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, issue a demand letter to the persons who had already retired from the government service and require them to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances.
10. We furthermore recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to locate the documents supporting the receivables for overpayment to suppliers and other government agencies, make representation with these entities for the furnishing of documents that could aid in the verification of the receivables, and prepare the demand for collection or the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Management still had outstanding unliquidated cash advances. Management still had to locate the supporting documents of the receivables.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the Loans Payable-Domestic account and coordinate with NEA to reconcile and update records maintained by both parties. Discrepancies noted should be reconciled, and the necessary adjustments effected, to ensure a fair presentation of the account in the financial statements. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to FS the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.
observations.managementAction: Management had not yet reconciled this loan with NEA. Also, they had not received any demand letters from NEA.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended that Management take immediate action to register the emergency rescue vehicle with the HFSRB pursuant to DOH AO No. 2018-001 dated January 28, 2018, to enable the Municipality to rectify the existing non-compliance and ensure that the vehicle be exclusively used for response and rescue/relief activities.
observations.managementAction: There was no designated focal person for the registration/accreditation of the vehicle.

observations.recommendation: 15. We recommended that Management desist from charging the expenses mentioned above to the LDRRMF and henceforth observe strictly the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013, and DILG MC No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012, on the proper utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that in CY 2023, the Municipality had questionable disbursements charged to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that Management ensure that the LDRRM motor vehicles be exclusively used for disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations in conformity with DILG MC No. 2021-004 dated January 18, 2021.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that in CY 2023, the vehicles had still been used for purposes other than rescue and relief operations.

observations.recommendation: 17. We recommended that the MDRRMO and the Property Officer require the drivers of the LDRRM vehicles to accomplish properly the prescribed form for the Driver’s Trip Tickets to ensure that all the data needed to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed for the period and the necessity of the trips undertaken are provided.
observations.managementAction: Some drivers had not provided adequate information about their trips.

observations.recommendation:
18. We recommended that before any repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles are undertaken, the personnel in charge shall be required to prepare the following reports, namely: a) pre-repair evaluation report and approved detailed plans showing in sufficient detail the scope of work/extent of repair to be done, b) report of waste materials, and c) document indicating the history of repair.
19. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements for the repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles are supported with the foregoing required documents plus a post-inspection report as required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The inspection reports had not been fully filled-up or numbered. There had also been no history of repair attached to the disbursements covering payment of repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation:
20. We recommended that Management submit the following documents to support the payment of honoraria to the members of the MCT and MIAC:
a) Executive Order creating the MCT and MIAC; b) Justification that the work rendered by the members of the MCT and MIAC was in addition to or over and above their regular workload; and c) Computation of the honoraria paid to the payees.
21. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management strictly comply with all the conditions stipulated under DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007, before honoraria of personnel are granted due to assignment in special government projects.
observations.managementAction: Management had already submitted the following documents: 1. Executive Order creating the MCT and MIAC; a. Executive Order creating the MCT and MIAC; 2. Certification that the work rendered by the Municipality’s Personnel and Officials was in addition to or over and above their regular workload as it pertains to a Special Project and that any honorarium received by the respective personnel was based on the actual service rendered in relation to such Special Project, signed by the KC-NCDDP AF Area Coordinator and HRMO-Designate and noted by the Municipal Mayor. However, the computation of honoraria paid to the employees had not been submitted.

observations.recommendation:
22. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer see to it that sufficient and vital information such as specific boundaries, stationing, landmarks, and symbols are provided in the Programs of Work, plans, and drawings for projects of road concreting and electrification of municipal streets.
23. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management unfailingly ensure strict compliance to the required detailed engineering activities in accordance with the provisions under Annex A of the RIRR of RA 9184, giving utmost consideration to the deficiencies discussed above.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer had submitted a justification which states that due to lack of personnel and budget, they were unable to complete the needed details.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommended that Management properly identify the GAD issues and concerns and use the Fund in order to implement programs, projects, and activities that would address specific gender gaps and are aligned with PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The GPB had already been approved so the Municipality is confident that the PPAs are compliant.

observations.recommendation:
25. We recommended that the Municipality, through the MSWDO, apprise the DSWD, RO No. VII of the beneficiaries listed under the UCT program who were already deceased, and recommend for possible replacement from among the eligible family members of the deceased.
26. We also recommended that Management ensure systematic procedures while strictly adhering to existing guidelines set forth under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020 in the implementation of SAP.
observations.managementAction: Possible replacements for the deceased beneficiaries had yet to be recommended. Management is also hoping for a reconsideration for the JO Workers since they belong to indigent families as well and the contract with the Municipality does not create an employer-employee relationship.

observations.recommendation: 27. We recommended that Management require the personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to completely and properly fill-up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Some POs in CY 2023 had still not been completely and properly filled out with the relevant and required information.

observations.recommendation: 32. We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Health Officer submit to the Auditor the following documents to substantiate the grant of SRA to the ten PHWs: a) Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious Diseases Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace; b) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well as the entitlement of the SRA; c) Individual approved daily time record or daily report of attendance indicating the dates and time they physically reported for work; and d) Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs quarantined in the Municipality’s COVID-19 facility, in the homes, and in other isolation facilities covering the same period of the ECQ.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the authority from the head of agency/ office to render service during the ECQ period, as well as some payroll documents pertaining to Hazard Pay. However, there had been no IATF Resolution on the Risk Certification in the workplace submitted. Also, there had been no DTRs attached to the DVs covering the payment of Hazard Pay because only the payroll was attached.

observations.recommendation: 41. Require the Municipal Engineer to conduct the complete detailed engineering activities before any POW is prepared and subsequently approved to ensure that projects are completed on schedule, the desired benefits are immediately enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries; 42. Closely monitor project implementation so that any unnecessary delays may be avoided; and 43. Immediately submit the suspension orders and resumption orders issued relative to the project Construction of Water Supply System at Sitio Bato, Barangay Basak together with the contractor’s requests for extension and justification, and the Municipal Engineer’s investigation reports used as basis in approving the request for suspensions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office had lacked a skilled operator for Autocad, hence they were unable to complete the needed details. Documents had yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: 46. We recommended that Management require the issuance of ORs (AF No. 51) from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. - Negros Oriental Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that the Municipality had already printed receipts of the Organizations instead of AF No. 51. In May 2018, the PCL’s Executive Director had sent a letter to the National Printing Office (NPO) for possible purchase of Accountable Form (AF) No. 51 and was informed that the concern was forwarded to the NPO Legal Team for the resolution of their request. The said letter also mentioned that at a meeting between PCL and COA National Office, an understanding was reached that the PCL will continue to use their receipts until the matter is resolved.

observations.recommendation: 47. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI on the CY 2018 AAR, and assign personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the Auditor. Henceforth, Management should strictly comply with the submission of the AAPSI within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that the AAPSI had not been submitted within the reglementary period. In fact, the AAPSI on the CY 2022 AAR was submitted by Management on November 20, 2023 or 143 days after they received the AAR on June 30, 2023.

observations.recommendation: 48. We recommended that, henceforth, the LDRRM Officer prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the report to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMO had not submitted the monthly report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund due to lack of personnel to handle and monitor the report.

observations.recommendation: 49. We recommended that Management submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 4.0 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated February 12, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2023 Annual GAD Plan and Budget had still not been received by the proper reviewing authorities within the prescribed periods.

observations.recommendation: 54. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaign in every barangay and by sending Notices of Delinquencies to all delinquent taxpayers and for the Municipality to avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already sent notices to delinquent taxpayers. However, some taxpayers had already transferred residence, while others could no longer be located. Also, those who were served with tax delinquency notices were unwilling to settle their tax duties.

observations.recommendation: 56. We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor with a copy of contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The copies of perfected contracts had not been submitted within the reglementary period and not all copies of perfected contracts had been submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: 57. We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of PPAs which are responsive to the objective of providing protection to children against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence and promotion of their rights and welfare as envisioned under R.A. 9344.
observations.managementAction: Verification of records showed that in CY 2023, only ₱660,934.54 or 50.50% of the total appropriations amounting to ₱1,308,772.49 had been utilized.

observations.recommendation: 58. We recommended that Management prioritize the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Management had prioritized the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill. A municipality had already entered into a MOA with LGU Bayawan for the disposal of municipal residual wastes. However, the Municipality still had to establish a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: 59. We recommended that the Municipality insure the project with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any damage to, or loss of the project in case fire, earthquake, storm, or other calamity.
observations.managementAction: Management had been confused as to the type and/or nature of government projects to be insured. Also, they needed significant amount of budget to cover the necessary cost of insurance premiums. Management had not yet complied with the GSIS set of requirements for the insurance of infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: 60. We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the prescribed time frame on the submission of accounts to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Management still lacked capable personnel to handle the task. Also, the preparation of the reports was done manually which added to the cause of delay.

observations.recommendation: 61. We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require the former OIC-District Supervisor to return the properties listed in Annex 16, including the one (1) unit 4WD, E-rider Recon Suzuki, otherwise, the latter should be required to pay the money value of these properties.
observations.managementAction: One (1) unit 4WD, E-rider Recon Suzuki had been left at the district, as per letter response submitted by Management. There was no cancelled Acknowledgment Receipt (ARE) considering that when the property was assigned to DepEd Zamboanguita, it was only assigned generally to DepEd in the Original ARE. However, the return of the properties other than the one (1) unit 4WD, E-rider Recon Suzuki had not been supported by any documents.

observations.recommendation: 62. We recommended that Management require the Scholarship Committee to require the applicants to submit the required report cards in 4th Year or previously completed year in tertiary level, proof of residency and the LGU to also require the submission of certificates of eligibility duly signed by the Chairman of the Committee pursuant to Sections 9, 10 and 12 of the Local Executive Order No. 14.
observations.managementAction: The scholarship program had been stopped but the previous disbursements for the scholarships were still not supported by the required documents. Management requested for reconsideration on the supporting documents considering it is no longer possible for them to provide the necessary documentation after a lapse of seven years.

observations.recommendation: 64. We recommended that the Municipality (i) require NGOs/POs to submit the required three-year period financial statement, (ii) release the amount of financial assistance as stipulated in the Work and Financial Plan (WFP) of the Municipality for the year, (iii) require the five (5) associations to submit lists of beneficiaries as an addendum to the MOA, (iv) monitor and inspect project implementation and verify financial records and reports of the NGOs/POs; and (v) require the liquidation of funds granted.
observations.managementAction: Although the Municipality had already stopped the granting of further financial support, the amounts already released still had to be accounted for. Non-submission of the reports had been an issue.

observations.recommendation: 65. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office conduct a regular count of the accountable forms in order to confirm the existence of the forms as reported in the Consolidated Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and reconcile with the subsidiary records maintained by the Accounting Office to ensure the correctness of the account balance shown in the financial statements at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Management had not conducted a physical count of the accountable forms. The volume of accountable forms and lack of personnel to handle the inventory had been cited.

observations.recommendation: 71. It was recommended that the Local Chief Executive refund the questioned financial assistance granted to constituents from other municipalities and comply with DBM SARO No. G-12-01378 on the limitation of the grant of financial assistance to residents of Zamboanguita only. Furthermore, the Municipal Social Welfare Office must see to it that the supporting documents required pursuant to the Omnibus Guidelines on the Management of DSWD-Operated Crisis Intervention Units are attached to the claims for payment.
observations.managementAction: Management found it hard to refund the amount since the individuals who were granted assistance could no longer be located. Management believed that some of the recipients were deserving indigent individuals although they came from other municipalities within the congressional district. Reconsideration was requested due to a lapse of more than 8 years to refund.

observations.recommendation: 72. We recommended that the payment of honorarium to BIR Collection Agents stationed in the Municipality be immediately stopped and the amounts already paid be refunded by the personnel concerned.
observations.managementAction: It is no longer possible to locate those individuals. Also, they were no longer connected with the BIR or any government agency and nobody knew their whereabouts.

observations.recommendation: 79. We recommended that all municipal officials who participated in the transactions, from the requisitioning to the approving officials, shall refund the total amount of the procured food items, being jointly and solidarily liable therefor.
observations.managementAction: An appeal for reconsideration had been forwarded to COA Regional Office VII relative to the disallowance dated February 26, 2015.

observations.recommendation: 81. Require the municipal accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers representing loans granted to qualified individuals under the Self-Employment Assistance Program of the DSWD. Likewise, require him to reconcile the Other Receivables – SEA Loan account with the available accounting records so that this can be supported with reliable balances of the persons granted with loans.
observations.managementAction: Management had been validating previous records as these were not systematically done and kept in prior years. However, the reconciliation of the subsidiary ledger of each recipient/ beneficiary was still not completed in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: 82. Require the Committee on the Inventory to prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) pursuant to Section 59 of the New Government Accounting System Manual, Volume II as basis in taking up the dropping of the properties from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Unavailability of records for the subject properties. Previous Management had not prepared and submitted the IIRUP because the personnel concerned had voluminous workload.

observations.recommendation: 84. Required the Municipal Accountant to update the submission of the trial balances and the required schedules to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 70 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Vol. I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was still applying the manual accounting system. Reports required from the Treasurer’s Office were not submitted immediately after the close of every quarter. Some of the personnel who were supposed to be assigned in the eNGAS had transferred to another agency, thus, the plan could not be materialized to date.

observations.recommendation: 89. Directed the Treasury, Accounting, and Assessor Offices to secure the Land Titles of all real properties as an indispensable requirement to establish true ownership of said properties. Lands owned or acquired by local governments without Torrens Title shall within the next fiscal year from their acquisition be surveyed with a view to securing title thereto, and the appropriation for such survey and other requirements for securing title shall be deemed against the unappropriated funds of such Municipality during the said ensuing fiscal year. All purchases of real property should be done in accordance with the pertinent laws governing such transaction in order to prevent future disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The titles for three remaining lots had yet to be secured.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts by strictly following the guidelines and
procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to
have reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and
accountability.

2.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Reclassify the cost of four units of brand-new dump trucks erroneously
recorded to asset account Motor Vehicles (1-07-06-010) to the proper
account Construction and Heavy Equipment (1-07-05-080) by making the
following adjusting entries:

Construction and Heavy Equipment ₱10,269,674.00
Motor Vehicles ₱10,269,674.00

b) Record the corresponding depreciation expense accounts used in recording
the annual depreciation expense for the dump trucks to be reclassified;

c) Transfer the unexpended and unobligated balances of the QRF and the
LDRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund be recorded in the General
Fund books at the end of the year as Transfers of Unspent Current Year
DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (5-02-15-010);

d) Reclassify the recorded cash advances from the asset account Advances for
Payroll (1-03-05-020) to the appropriate account Advances to Special
Disbursing Officer (1-03-05-030) by making the following adjusting entry:

Advances to Special Disbursing Officer (1-03-05-030)₱1,638,341.25
Advances for Payroll (1-03-05-020) ₱1,638,341.25

e) Rectify the corresponding liquidation of the cash advances that were
previously credited to the account Advances for Payroll (1-03-05-020); and

f) Refer to the description of accounts as a guide in recording expenses in the
books of accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December
1, 2015, to avoid erroneous entries.

3.10. We recommended that Management submit the following documents to
support the payment of honoraria to the members of the MCT and MIAC:
a. Performance evaluation plan formulated by project management used as a
basis for rating the performance of the members of the MCT and MIAC;
b. Special Project Plan;
c. Certificate of completion of project deliverables;
d. Certificate of acceptance by the agency head of the deliverables per project
component;
e. Documentary evidence showing the following:
i. number of man-hours each member of the MCT and MIAC spent on the
project beyond their regular working hours;
ii. nature of the member’s work assignments and the level of difficulty of the
duties and responsibilities assigned;
iii. extent of their productivity and quality of performance measured in
terms of completed and accepted deliverables in accordance with the
timeframes set; and
iv. performance rating given by the project management; and
f. Computation of the honoraria paid to the payees.

3.11. We also recommended that, henceforth, Management strictly comply with all the
conditions stipulated under DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated
October 1, 2007, before honoraria of personnel are granted due to assignment in
special government projects.

4.8. We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the provisions
of R.A. No. 7305 and its revised IRR for the grant of hazard pay to its PHWs.
We further recommended that Management secure and submit to the audit
team the documentary requirements for the payment of hazard pay to the PHWs
as listed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

5.7. We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor appoint or
designate an additional DO to avoid granting multiple cash advances to the
incumbent DO. We also recommended that no additional cash advances be
granted to the DO unless the previous cash advance is settled or properly
accounted for.

6.15. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the AOs monitor the
effectivity and expiration dates of their respective fidelity bonds and see to it that
renewal thereof be made before the expiration.

6.16. We further recommended that, henceforth, no AO shall be allowed to perform
their duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that
cash advances granted to AOs do not exceed the maximum accountability
covered by their fidelity bonds to protect the interest of the Municipal
government.

7.15. We recommended that Management review the Municipality’s functions,
systems and procedures, organizational structure, and staffing pattern to
address any needs and gaps in the human resource complement.

7.16. We also recommended that Management comply with all the provisions of the
CSC-COA-DBM JC No. 1, s. of 2017, as amended, and provide for wages of

We recommended that Management:

a) Instruct the Station Manager to require all clients of the Radyo
Kaigsoonan to pay the required fees at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office
and present the OR (AF 51) at the Station before airing any
advertisements to ensure that these are recorded as government funds
and subjected to government accounting and auditing rules and
regulations;

b) Make all payment of expenses for the operation of the Station by check
except for petty expenses which should be charged against the Petty Cash
Fund;

c) Submit to the Auditor the following: (i) Latest annual reports submitted
to NNC with proof of receipt by the NNC; and (ii) List of
clients/subscribers together with the corresponding contract, if there is
any; and

d) Submit an explanation on the deficiencies noted.

Management explained that the enacted ordinance intended to regulate the
imposition of fees was not implemented due to the objection of NNC, highlighting the
fact that the Radio Station was not designed for commercial operation, but
exclusively for public service. They added that the amounts received by the Radio
Station Management were donations from avid listeners and some store owners which
they recorded in their logbook. They also stated that effective July 1, 2021, they no
longer accepted payments/donations. Management further stated that they are now
weighing the advantages and disadvantages of running the Radio Station. If NNC will
continue to object the imposition of fees, there is no way that the Municipality can
improve and guarantee the continuity of the service considering that all radio anchors
were hired on a job order basis while some were volunteers. According to
Management, it could not be denied that the Radio Station had helped the
Municipality in its continued fight against COVID-19, and in the delivery of basic
information especially for nutirition and health development.
The Auditor would like to emphasize that the Radio Station Management was
not prohibited from receiving donations. In fact, as provided in Item II.A.6 of the
MOA, the NNC shall allow the creation of Marketing Arm for the solicitation of
foreign and local grants, donations and advertisements.
It bears stressing, as well, that the main concern of the Auditor was for the
collections of the Radio Station to be acknowledged by official receipts (AF 51) to
ensure that these are recorded as government funds and subjected to government
accounting and auditing rules and regulations. Even if the collections were merely
donations from avid listeners and some store owners, Management is reminded that
since these were received by and for the Radio Station which is owned and operated
by the LGU, it is only proper that these should be treated and accounted for as
government funds.

benefits are immediately enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries;

b) Closely monitor project implementation so that any unnecessary delays
may be avoided; and

c) Immediately submit the suspension orders and resumption orders issued
relative to the project Construction of Water Supply System at Sitio Bato,
Barangay Basak together with the contractor’s requests for extension and
justification, and the Municipal Engineer’s investigation reports used as
basis in approving the request for suspensions.

Management agreed with the recommendations. On the other hand, the
Municipal Engineer explained that the delay of the the project Construction of Water
Supply System at Sitio Bato, Barangay Basak was caused by insufficiency of funds.
He admitted that the Detailed Engineering Designs and other required surveys were
not conducted because a significant amount was required for the services. Since the
project had been started, they exerted effort to find ways to complete it.
The Auditor would like to remind the Management that it is their principal
responsibility to ensure that the required detailed engineering activities are done
before the preparation and subsequent approval of POWs so that the programmed
funds are sufficient for the project. This is also a way to ensure completion of projects
within the programmed time frame. By so doing, deficiencies such as the ones
discussed in the subject audit finding could be avoided.

We recommended that the Municipal personnel who are still employed in
the Municipality be required to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash
advances, otherwise the sanction of withholding of honoraria provided under
existing COA rules and regulations will have to be imposed.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Accountant, issue a demand letter to the persons who had already retired from the
government service and require them to immediately settle their unliquidated cash
advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate
additional DO to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to the incumbent
DO.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer
as well as the Accountable Officers monitor the effectivity and expiration dates
of their respective fidelity bonds and see to it that renewal thereof be made
before their expiration. Henceforth, no Accountable Officer shall be allowed to
perform their duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure
that cash advances granted to AOs do not exceed the maximum accountability
covered by their fidelity bonds in order to protect the interest of the Municipal
government.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of
ORs (AF No. 51) from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. - Negros Oriental
Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We
further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the
leagues comply with this requirement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all department
heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI on the CY
2018 AAR, and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the
Auditor. Henceforth, Management should strictly comply with the submission
of the AAPSI within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years.

We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference
between the RPT and SET balances totaling ₱4,263,515.17 and, henceforth,
conduct, together with the Municipal Assessor, a periodic reconciliation of
the balances of Basic and Special Education Tax dues and RPT Receivable and SET
Receivable accounts to avoid accumulation of unreconciled items;
b) The Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an
assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have
been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties, on or
before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year;
c) The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of
taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as basis for
recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in compliance with
Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
d) The Municipal Accountant maintain SLs for control accounts RPT Receivable
and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume 1I.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of
delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaign in every
barangay and by sending Notices of Delinquencies to all delinquent taxpayers
and for the Municipality to avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real
property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist establish the actual
status of the breeding stocks under the dispersal program by tracing the
whereabouts and condition of the original breeding stocks and their offspring, if
any, and render a report to the Municipal Accountant which should include the
fair market value of each stock, for proper recording in the books of accounts.
In case of death/loss of assets, necessary adjusting entries shall be taken only
after the issuance of a credit notice arising from the result of the evaluation of
the relief from accountability, if any.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor
with a copy of contracts together with the supporting documents within the
reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with
Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that all documentary requirements for the payment of
expenses in the implementation of development projects enumerated under
Sections 9.1 to 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 be
immediately submitted to the Auditor. We further recommended that,
henceforth, subsequent disbursements relative to infrastructure projects should
be completely substantiated by the requisite supporting documents.

We recommended that the MDRRM Council include in its LDRRMFIP
the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years
pursuant to Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012. We further recommended that the MDRRM Council take
a concerted effort to plan and implement pre-disaster preparedness PPAs
charged to prior year’s unexpended LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, maximize the
use of the 70% LDRRMF by implementing programs/projects/activities that
would eliminate, lessen or limit adverse impacts of hazards and related disasters
or calamities as enumerated in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and DILG Memorandum Circular
No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) immediately stop using
the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not properly chargeable to the Fund; (b)
henceforth, include in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan PPAs that
conform to the PPAs enumerated in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.
2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and DILG MC No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012;
and (c) the amount of ₱536,505.64 representing a portion of the LDRRMF
appropriations spent for PPAs which are not properly chargeable thereto
should be restored and refunded from the appropriations of the GF Proper.

We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, the
Municipality properly identify the GAD issues and concerns and learn the use
of the Fund in order to implement programs, projects and activities which are
aligned with PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.

We recommended that management, through the MPDC, fast track the
establishment of the GAD database storage of necessary information in aid of
effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in
accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the
PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended
beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that
may be provided.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that management undertake a concerted effort to fast
track the implementation of PPAs outlined under the 20% DF so that the
constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the projects.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that management prioritize the implementation of PPAs
which are responsive to the objective of providing protection to children against
abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence and promotion of their rights and welfare
as envisioned under R.A. 9344.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the LSB refrain from charging expenses not listed
under Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and its related DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint
Circular, review the PPAs included in the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization
thereof, and for the Municipal Accountant to secure from the DepEd Accountant a
certification that request on the use of SEF is net of the budgetary provision for the
same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd and the ECCD Council.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that management prioritize the establishment and
operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003
and meantime,take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled
dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended
that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and
segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the contractor be required to complete the project as soon as possible and to impose sanctions and penalties, if warranted, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: the Municipality terminated the contract through BAC Resolution No. 03-2018 and awarded the remaining works to qualified contractor through negotiated contract.
observations.reason: Due to the unreasonable period of delay, and after several extensions given, the Municipality terminated the contract through BAC Resolution No. 03-2018 and awarded the remaining works to qualified contractor through negotiated contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality insure the project with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any damage to, or loss of the project in case fire, earthquake, storm, or other calamity.
observations.managementAction: Verified with GSIS Management on the set of requirements for availing the set by the GSIS. The Municipality informed the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Due to the unreasonable loss of the project in case of calamity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly with the Specific Implementation Agreement of the General Memorandum of Agreement and the Implementation Plan and Arrangement of the Project to avoid audit disallowance. We also recommended that a written explanation/justification be submitted to the auditor as to why the extension of the seed capital should not constitute an election offense as well as justification on the following deficiencies: a. Delayed extension of Seed Capital; b. Beneficiaries who are not NHTS/Pantawid members; c. Inequitable distribution of capital assistance; d. Absence of monitoring and coordination.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality saw to it that further implementation of the Seed Capital Project was strictly in accordance with the guidelines set by MLGU, DSWD, and other agencies. The following are the justifications on the deficiencies noted: 1. Supporting documents submitted to DSWD RFO7; 2. List of beneficiaries submitted to DSWD RFO7; 3. All pantawid beneficiaries in ten barangays were all prioritized in the regular program of the sustainable livelihood of DSWD. SLP worker had already organized an association; 4. Conducted a meeting in every barangay and coordinated with the barangay officials for the collection of the beneficiaries’ obligations. Replacement report was re-submitted and was subsequently approved.
observations.reason: The Municipality saw to it that further implementation of the Seed Capital Project was strictly in accordance with the guidelines set by MLGU, DSWD, and other agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the OIC-Office of the Assistant Regional Director and Concurrent Schools Division Superintendent seek the approval of the DepEd Secretary on the establishment of the extension schools to avoid the wastage of funds used to pay for the honoraria of Teacher Aides.
observations.managementAction: The District-in-Charge of OIC-Municipal Supervisor required the former OIC-District Supervisor to return the properties listed in Annex 16, including the one (1) unit 4WD, E-rider Suzuki, otherwise, the latter should be required to pay the money value of these properties. The current District-in-Charge made an indorsement addressed to Mr. Cimafranca, OIC-District Supervisor, for the action regarding the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Awaiting the required turn-over documents especially the one unit 4WD, E-rider Recon Suzuki.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management desist from spending government funds for expenses which do not benefit the municipality and henceforth, adhere strictly to Sections 335 and 343 of R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality discontinued the payment of honorarium to the Collection Agent, and other amounts already paid be refunded by the personnel concerned.
observations.reason: Fiesta-related expenses were not charged to government funds henceforth, adhere strictly to Sections 335 and 343 of R.A. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that heads of offices prepare a Project Procurement Management Plan for the different programs, projects and activities of their offices, and for the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to consolidate the same in the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with Section 7.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the required Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and Annual Procurement Plan (APP).
observations.reason: Scheduled turn-over was observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still opted to procure common-use supplies from locally authorized suppliers because of the distance between the LGU and the nearest available PS-DBM depot, and the uncertainty of the availability of the needed supplies.
observations.reason: Lack of availability of the needed supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to adhere strictly to the prescribed time frame on the submission of the accounts to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Submitted reports on a quarterly basis. Management lacked personnel and the use of manual procedures in preparing the reports.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and the use of manual procedures in preparing the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Transition Team conduct an inventory as soon as possible, prepare and submit the report of physical count as of June 30, 2016 to the incumbent Municipal Mayor pursuant to Item B.1 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21.
observations.managementAction: The Local Governance Transition Team was created. Scheduled turn-over was observed.
observations.reason: Scheduled turn-over was observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the former OIC-District Supervisor be required to return the properties listed in Annex 16, including the one (1) unit 4WD, E-rider Suzuki, otherwise, the latter should be required to pay the money value of these properties. Xx
observations.managementAction: Current District-in-Charge made an indorsement addressed to Mr. Cimafranca, former OIC-District Supervisor, for the action regarding the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Awaiting the required turn-over documents especially the one unit 4WD, E-rider Recon Suzuki.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive appropriate one percent (1%) of its internal revenue allotment and utilize the same for programs, projects and activities that would safeguard and protect the welfare of children in accordance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated exactly the required amount equivalent to 1% of the IRA in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive refund the questioned financial assistance granted to constituents from other municipalities and comply with DBM SARO No. G-12-01378 on the limitation of the grant of financial assistance to residents of Zamboanguita only. Further, require the Municipal Social Welfare Office to see to it that the supporting documents are attached to the claims for payment.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to adhere strictly to the guidelines set in the future releases of funds, if any.
observations.reason: The financial assistance was done in good faith and the constituents believed that they were recipients though some were from other municipalities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 on the preparation and submission of the duly accomplished Trip Ticket and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels so that the reasonableness of the consumption of fuel and the use of municipal vehicle can be easily established.
observations.managementAction: The LCE appointed Mr. David dela Peña as dispatcher of all usage of municipal vehicles. Also, trip tickets were required from all drivers to be properly filled up and submitted on time, otherwise, no value of gasoline would be approved in the succeeding trip.
observations.reason: Failed to submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials require the five (5) associations to submit the required three-year period financial statement, submit lists of beneficiaries as an addendum to the MOA, monitor and inspect project implementation and verify financial records and reports of the NGOs/POs, and require liquidation of funds granted.
observations.managementAction: The recipient NGOs/POs were informed and considering that they were in operation for less than three years, the required three-year statements were only for the year prior to the grant. Some of the required 3-year financial statements were not available because they were in operation for less than three years.
observations.reason: Some of the required 3-year financial statements were not available considering that they were in operation for less than three years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office conduct a regular count of the accountable forms in order to confirm the existence of the forms as reported in the Consolidated Monthly Report of Accountability of Accountable Forms and reconcile with the subsidiary records maintained by the Accounting Office to ensure the correctness of the account balance shown in the financial statements at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor informed the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to maintain the accountable forms and to reconcile with the records. The Municipal Treasurer issued directives and a checking process was ordered by the ICO-Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Some were not able to submit the required documentation and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU appoint a Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (DRRMO) and three (3) staffs pursuant to Section 2, Rule 6 of Republic Act No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO commented that the MDRRMO position and three (3) staff are being substituted by job order personnel due to financial constraints/limitations.
observations.reason: Being substituted by job orders for the time being due to financial constraints/limitations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations and to locate any missing assets. Before any assets acquired in prior years be demolished, inform proper offices of any public structures due to demolition and submit the required documentation. The Municipal Accountant to reclassify fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from PPE to Other Asset accounts and to locate disbursement vouchers of the unbooked medical equipment and record the same.
observations.managementAction: The members of the Inventory Committee are occupying key functions in the Municipality and also, the voluminous workload. The Municipal Engineer informed proper offices of any public structures due to demolition. The necessary documentary requirements were forwarded to the auditor. The Municipal Accountant to reclassify and take up the assets.
observations.reason: The members of the Inventory Committee are also occupying key functions in the Municipality and the voluminous workload made it difficult to complete the inventory even if it was conducted by barangay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials concerned remit the unexpended balances of cash received from the national government and PDAF to the source agency/National Treasury as required.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality issued Check No. 1076998 amounting to ₱894,310.68, but it was later cancelled. The Municipal Treasurer was required to indicate the specific sources of funds in the schedule of cash returned, which he failed to do so.
observations.reason: The unexpended PDAF amount was not remitted to the Bureau of National Treasury because the Municipal Treasurer failed to indicate the specific sources of funds in the schedule of cash returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive refund the questioned financial assistance granted to constituents from other municipalities and comply with DBM SARO No. G-12-01378 on the limitation of the grant of financial assistance to residents of Zamboanguita only. Furthermore, require the Municipal Social Welfare Office to attach the supporting documents to the claims for payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor promised to adhere strictly to the guidelines set in the future releases of funds, if any.
observations.reason: The Municipality believed that the grant was done in good faith and the constituents believed that they were beneficiaries, although some were from other municipalities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the payment of honorarium to BIR Collection Agents stationed in the Municipality be immediately stopped and amounts already paid be refunded by the personnel concerned.
observations.managementAction: Discontinued the payment of honorarium to the BIR Collection Agents stationed in the Municipality and other amounts already paid be refunded by the personnel concerned. Also, the Collection Agent, Jose Orestes Villaranda, is no longer connected specifically with the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dumpsite by: 1. Fencing the perimeter area; 2. Assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling; and 3. Implement the Solid Waste Management Program as soon as possible and request the Sangguniang Bayan for a Supplemental Budget for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: The dump site is fenced but is estimated to cover only 50% of the whole area, thus it is not secured from trespassers and animal entry. The LGU lacked adequate funds for the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Program.
observations.reason: There was no efficient garbage disposal system in place because the wastes were not segregated into biodegradable and non-biodegradable types and the LGU lacked adequate funds for the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be instructed to intensify real property tax collections and that the Local Finance Committee study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenue sources and fees to minimize the municipality’s dependence on IRA.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2017, real property tax collections were based on the newly approved general revision rates, thus, the increase in revenue generation of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Local Finance Committee is yet to find ways to further increase generation of revenue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Maintain and update the RPTAR/TIC and iTax regularly; b. Require the municipal treasurer at the beginning of each year to generate the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount of real property taxes due and collectible for the year from the iTax which shall serve as the basis of the municipal accountant to record the real property tax and special education tax receivables as required in Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I; c. Henceforth, implement strictly Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The personnel charged with the generation of the RPTAR/TIC and iTax were no longer in the service. Management is looking for another person capable of handling the iTax program and generating the report.
observations.reason: Personnel charged with the generation of the RPTAR/TIC and iTax were no longer in the service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a. Conduct a Physical Inventory of all the involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets; b. Require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the General Fund or to its related Registries; c. Maintain a properly accomplished/updated Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Bookkeeper was in the process of preparing the list of properties due for transfer to the General Fund or to its related Registries.
observations.reason: Management had difficulty verifying the existence of the properties since some were constructed several years ago and some MOAs could not be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Exert extra effort to verify from the Memorandum of Agreement relative to the treatment of the excess funds. If no provisions are available, management should request approval from the source agencies to use the money for other projects or activities of the LGU. Otherwise, the funds should be remitted to the National Treasury; b. Henceforth, regular review of the Due to NGAS accounts should be effected by the Municipal Accountant and concerned offices to ensure that funds are utilized as planned.
observations.managementAction: Management verified the existence of the MOA in order to validate the funds were granted, and the project to which the fund was released. Some of the MOAs could not be located as they were granted several years ago.
observations.reason: Some of the MOAs could not be located as the funds were granted several years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require refund through payroll deduction and in the event of separation or retirement, full deduction of the outstanding balance from the terminal pay unless the employee retires, wherein, the entire amount will be deducted.
observations.managementAction: Awaiting the decision from COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials who participated in the transactions, from the requisitioning to the approving officials, shall refund the total amount of the procured food items and the refunding officials be held jointly and solidarily liable.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor implemented directives and required the officials to refund the amounts where appropriate.
observations.reason: Decision pending from COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We required the municipal officials concerned to submit copies of the delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance of goods and services thereof, to enable the auditor to inspect, review and evaluate the transactions/projects.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the delivery documents were submitted by the LGU to the auditor via alternative modes, however the required submission was not regularly observed.
observations.reason: The required submission of documents was not regularly observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers representing loans granted to qualified individuals under the Self-Employment Assistance Program of the DSWD. Likewise, require him to reconcile the Other Receivables – SEA Loan account with the available accounting records so that this can be supported with reliable balances of the persons granted with loans.
observations.managementAction: Directed the Bookkeeper to maintain the subsidiary ledgers and reconcile the Other Receivables – SEA Loan account with the available accounting records.
observations.reason: Previous records were not systematically done and kept.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Committee on Inventory prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to identify the unserviceable properties and prepare the report thereon to serve as basis in taking up the dropping of the properties from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor instructed the Committee on Inventory to prepare and submit the IIRUP which was carried out.
observations.reason: The personnel in-charge had voluminous workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate refund in full of the extra cash gift amounting to ₱950,000.00 for CY 2009 and henceforth, ensure that the local chief executive or those participating in similar benefits comply with the prescribed PS limitation so that the disbursements will not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Refund through payroll deduction on a monthly basis on issuance of the required authorization. For those who retired or separated from the service, deduction of the full amount from terminal pay.

observations.recommendation: We required the Municipal Accountant to update the submission of the trial balances and the required schedules to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 70 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Vol. I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the required reports though beyond the reglementary period due to lack of personnel and manual preparation.
observations.reason: Reports were submitted beyond the reglementary period due to lack of personnel and manual preparation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the municipal agricultural officer to account all livestock and furnish the municipal accountant the corresponding list and its supporting documents to serve as basis in taking up accountability in the books of account and to maintain and update Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Property Card as required under Section 46 of the new Government Accounting System manual, Volume II and to prepare an Inventory Report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality started accounting for the livestock dispersal with the assistance of the farmer’s associations. Updating of property cards is going on.

observations.reason: Reconciliation is still going on due to the immensity of the work.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality started securing Land Titles of all real properties with the Registry of Deeds. Application for land titling is in process.
observations.reason: Application for land titling is still in process.

observations.managementAction: The LGU did not release assistance to NGOs/POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exert effort to verify unexpended balances and request source agencies for guidance; if no provision allow return to National Treasury; conduct regular review of Due to NGA accounts and remitting excess funds if appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The LGU tried to identify the sources and coordinate; some returns were processed and follow-ups made.
observations.reason: The Bureau of Treasury would not accept the deposit because the source agency was not specified in the remittance schedule; older records missing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all municipal officials who participated in the transactions refund the total amount of procured food items and observe guidelines for emergency assistance procurement.
observations.managementAction: Payment of questioned amounts was stopped and amounts were deducted from responsible personnel where applicable.
observations.reason: The Notice of Disallowance was under appeal.
