We recommended that management desist from spending government funds for expenses which do not benefit the municipality and henceforth, adhere strictly to Sections 335 and 343 of R.A. 7160.

We recommended and management agreed to require heads of offices to prepare a Project Procurement Management Plan for the different programs, projects activities of their offices, and for the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to consolidate the same in the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with Section 7.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended and the management agreed to procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
We recommended and management agreed to adhere strictly to the prescribed time frame on the submission of accounts to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommended that the Local Transition Team conduct an inventory as soon as possible, prepare and submit the report of physical count as of June 30, 2016 to the incumbent Municipal Mayor pursuant to Item B.1 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require the former OIC-District Supervisor to return the properties listed in Annex 16, including the one (1) unit 4WD, E-rider Recon Suzuki, otherwise, the latter should be required to pay the money value of these properties.

We recommended that Management appropriate one percent (1%) of its internal revenue allotment and utilize the same for programs, projects and activities that would safeguard and protect the welfare of children in accordance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that management adhere to the specific purpose contained in the NCA in the disbursement of funds. We further recommend that the Scholarship Committee require the applicants to submit the required supporting documents in accordance with Sections 9, 10 and 12 of Local Executive Order No. 14.
observations.managementAction: A letter was sent to DBM, through Cong. Henry Pryde Teves, seeking clarification on the disbursement of funds.
observations.reason: As of date, the municipality has not received a reply from Cong. Teves yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 on the preparation and submission of the duly accomplished Fuel Trip Ticket and Monthly Consumption Reports and Monthly Report of Official Travels so that the reasonableness of the consumption of fuel and the use of municipal vehicle can be easily established.
observations.managementAction: The LCE appointed Mr. David dela Peña as vehicle dispatcher who is in-charged of recording in the logbook the use of Official Travels.
observations.reason: Management overlooked the reporting of Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels due to the volume of workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require taxpayers to submit proof of gross sales/income when applying for renewal of business permits to ascertain the accuracy of the declared gross receipts subject to tax.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer started to require the taxpayers to present their books of accounts and the financial statements if available, as basis in determining their gross receipts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office conduct regular counts of accountable forms and reconcile with subsidiary records maintained by the Accounting Office to ensure the correctness of the account balance shown in the financial statements at year-end.
observations.managementAction: A letter and instructions were issued; the Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepared inventory reports from department submissions.
observations.reason: As of date, the committee members are preoccupied with their respective duties and full implementation has not been completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant see to it that expenses charged to GAD are within the annual GAD Plan and Budget. Henceforth, strict adherence to Executive Order No. 273 and other existing rules and regulations on GAD is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The municipality adjusted procedures and ensured future GAD disbursements will follow the GAD Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU appoint a Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (DRRMO) and three (3) staff pursuant to Section 2, Rule 6 of Republic Act No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The LCE appointed a DRRMO and one staff.
observations.reason: Only one (1) staff was appointed instead of three (3) staffs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and require the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with regulations, and to prepare appropriate documentation for demolition or transfer where applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that department heads submitted Reports of Physical Count based on assets found in their offices; stickers were affixed and inventory taking started.
observations.reason: A significant number of movable assets were acquired in prior years, making it difficult to complete the inventory taking; some supporting documentation and proper tagging still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that municipal officials remit the unexpended balances of funds received from the national government to the source agency/National Treasury as required, or if permitted request source agency approval to use otherwise return the funds.
observations.managementAction: The municipality processed return of funds; one check was issued but later cancelled; follow-up actions taken to identify sources.
observations.reason: Check No. 1076998 amounting to ₱894,310.68 was not remitted to the Bureau of Treasury because the municipality was required to indicate the specific source of funds in the schedule of cash returned.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that all municipal officials who participated in the transactions be required to refund the total amount of financial assistance granted to constituents of other municipalities and comply with DBM SARO and supporting document requirements.
observations.managementAction: Management sent a letter to DBM seeking proof/basis that it is lawful to provide assistance to constituents of other municipalities; MSWDO attached supporting documents to claims.
observations.reason: As of date, the municipality has not received a definitive reply from DBM/Congressional office; documentation follow-up ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and General Services Officer require that all municipal vehicles be marked 'FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY', that driver’s trip tickets be properly filled up, and that Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels be prepared to justify expenses.
observations.managementAction: All vehicles were marked 'FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY' and drivers instructed to complete trip tickets; measures implemented to improve reporting.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Social Welfare Officer/s to follow DSWD guidelines in purchasing foods for indigent children and submit written justification why fresh milk was purchased instead of hot meals; if not supported charge responsible officers/employees.
observations.managementAction: A written justification was made by the MSWDO, Ms. Helen Partosa.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that payment of honorarium to BIR Collection Agents stationed in the municipality be immediately stopped and amounts already paid be refunded by the personnel concerned.
observations.managementAction: Payment of honorarium to BIR Collection Agents was stopped.
observations.reason: Some amounts were refunded; one or more officers involved were no longer assigned in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: Recommend measures to mitigate the effects of the open dumpsite: fence the perimeter area; assign personnel to segregate wastes for recycling; implement Solid Waste Management Program; request supplemental budget and include appropriations for Solid Waste Program in annual budget; coordinate with nearby LGUs for sanitary landfill cluster.
observations.managementAction: The dumpsite was fenced. The municipality coordinated with neighboring LGUs about a cluster for a sanitary landfill. Supplemental budget actions were initiated.
observations.reason: Mines and Geosciences Bureau (MGB) has not acted yet on the request for assessment; cluster landfill planning ongoing; further actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Local Finance Committee explore possibilities to increase local revenue sources and intensify real property tax collections including General Revision of Real Property Valuation and reexamination of fees to minimize dependence on IRA.
observations.managementAction: Follow-ups made with Provincial Office for General Revision of Real Property Valuation; local measures to intensify collections were implemented.
observations.reason: General Revision of Real Property Valuation formulated but valuation start was delayed; collection improvements ongoing with full effect expected in subsequent years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management maintain and update the RPTAR/ITax regularly, require the municipal treasurer to generate certified lists of taxpayers and amounts at the beginning of each year to serve as basis for recording receivables.
observations.managementAction: Records were updated and personnel assigned; efforts made to generate certified lists from ITax.
observations.reason: Personnel to operate ITax program were being sought; updates and reconciliations completed where possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management conduct physical inventory of involved properties to determine existence and condition; require that properties under trust funds be transferred to General Fund accounts; maintain updated Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested the bookkeeper to provide exact breakdown and to locate related registries; some transfers to General Fund initiated.
observations.reason: Difficulty in verifying properties since some were constructed several years ago and related records are incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accountant trace supporting documents and give priority to clear this deficiency to eliminate the liability from the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Records were updated and efforts made to trace supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require personnel to refund the excess amounts and ensure future compliance with PS limitation and DBM circular.
observations.managementAction: Refunds were processed by deducting from monthly salaries of persons liable; adjustments made for retired employees.
observations.reason: Employees were charged and portions recovered; not all amounts fully settled.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal officials concerned to submit copies of delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance to enable the auditor to inspect and evaluate transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reminded personnel in charge of procurement to provide the auditor with copies of delivery documents; some improvements made.
observations.reason: Documents were constantly not submitted due to personnel shortages in procurement.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers representing loans granted and reconcile the Other Receivables – SEA Loan account with supporting records.
observations.managementAction: Accounting staff updated records where possible and reconciled amounts for those with available documentation.
observations.reason: Difficulty in locating records for some loan beneficiaries; reconciliations completed for available items.

observations.recommendation: Require the Committee on Inventory to prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property and take up the dropping of properties from the books as warranted.
observations.managementAction: The committee processed dispositions and prepared the required IIRUP reports.
observations.reason: The list of unserviceable properties was compiled and dispositions processed.

observations.recommendation: Immediately refund in full the extra cash gift amounting to ₱950,000.00 and ensure future compliance so disbursements will not be disallowed.
observations.managementAction: Payments of audit disallowance were deducted from monthly salaries of liable persons; retired employees had deductions from terminal benefits where applicable.
observations.reason: Not all amounts fully settled; some liabilities remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to update and submit trial balances and required schedules to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 70 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Accounting staff rendered overtime particularly at year-end to prepare and submit required reports.
observations.reason: Reports were prepared but submission was delayed due to lack of personnel and heavy year-end workload.

observations.recommendation: Direct the municipal agricultural officer to account all livestock and furnish the municipal accountant the corresponding lists and supporting documents to take up the same in the books; maintain and update Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Property Cards.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the Agricultural Officer to prepare supporting lists and documents; some records were provided.
observations.reason: Unavailability of supporting records for livestock acquired several years ago hindered full reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Treasurer, Assessor and Accounting Offices to reconcile records and secure land titles; Municipal Assessor to update records and facilitate titling of lots.
observations.managementAction: Assessor and accounting offices commenced reconciliation and data gathering to support titling and record updates.
observations.reason: Titling is still in process and some historical records are incomplete which delays full reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Treasurer, Accounting and Assessor Offices to secure Land Titles of all real properties and to reconcile and substantiate ownership; survey and initiate titling where warranted.
observations.managementAction: Applications for titles were processed and coordinated with the Registry of Deeds; some titles actions were initiated.
observations.reason: Issuance of Torrens Titles is still in process with the Registry of Deeds.
